metadata.title: Offshore Crustal Thickness Variation along the Palu-Koro Strike-Slip Fault in the Sulawesi region from OBS Receiver Function Analysis
metadata.study_area: Celebes Sea and Makassar Strait, straddling the Palu-Koro Fault zone
metadata.method: OBS H-κ stacking (receiver functions from teleseismic P waves) + neighbourhood algorithm waveform inversion; 9 OBSs + 1 land station (TOLI2); deployed Aug 2019 – Aug 2020
conclusions: Celebes Sea: thin oceanic crust ~8-12 km beneath OBSs; Moho depth ~12-17 km (including water column)
Makassar Strait: transitional/continental crust ~24-26 km (M01G–M03F stations), suggesting modified continental crust rather than purely oceanic
North Sulawesi (TOLI2): thickest crust ~30 km, consistent with ancient island arc terrane
PKF zone (C28F, 0.871°N, 119.766°E): 25.1 km Moho depth — pronounced step between this station and northern Celebes Sea stations confirms Moho offset across the fault

PKF is a deep, throughgoing lithospheric-scale strike-slip fault in the Celebes Sea, evidenced by: (1) low-velocity zone at C28F; (2) sharp Moho step; (3) relocated 2018 aftershocks aligning with fault trace
Back-projection of 2018 Mw 7.5 Palu earthquake: rapid NNW rupture propagation, then southward with ~20 s delay — asymmetric rupture behavior confirmed
Palu-Koro fault accommodates oblique convergence and trench retreat, and is a major lithospheric boundary separating Makassar Block (W) from North Sula Block (E)

properties.name: OBS C28F — PKF zone (Palu-Koro Fault offshore)
properties.tectonic_setting: Palu-Koro Fault zone offshore extension; transitional crust at the fault boundary
properties.interpretation: Relatively thick crust (22.7 km) compared to northern Celebes Sea OBSs. Sharp Moho step between this station (25.1 km) and C08F (17.3 km) to the north marks the PKF crustal boundary. Low-velocity zone at this site interpreted as fractured or partially serpentinized fault zone material extending to mid-lower crust.

properties.name: OBS C08F — Southern Celebes Sea
properties.tectonic_setting: Southern Celebes Sea, transitional (north of PKF zone)

properties.name: OBS C21F — Eastern Celebes Sea (thinnest crust)
properties.tectonic_setting: Eastern Celebes Sea oceanic crust; thinnest measured — purely oceanic character

properties.name: OBS M01G — Makassar Strait (continental crust)
properties.tectonic_setting: Makassar Strait; transitional/continental crust on western side of PKF

properties.name: OBS M03F — Makassar Strait (south PKF)
properties.tectonic_setting: Makassar Strait; just south of PKF transition zone. Sharp Moho step to C28F (just to north across PKF) confirms crustal boundary.

properties.name: Land Station TOLI2 — North Sulawesi (island arc crust)
properties.tectonic_setting: Northern Sulawesi (island arc); thickest measured crust in study. Low Vp/Vs (1.86) consistent with felsic/arc crust composition.
properties.interpretation: ~30 km crust reflects ancient island arc terrane of North Arm. Consistent with Greenfield et al. (2021) finding of continental-like lithosphere. Gradual crustal thickening from North Sulawesi Trench landward.

properties.name: Palu-Koro Fault — Offshore Extension into Celebes Sea
properties.evidence: Low-velocity zone at C28F depth profile; sharp Moho step between Makassar Strait (M02F/M03F, ~25 km) and Celebes Sea stations (C08F, 17.3 km); relocated 2018 Palu aftershocks; back-projection shows NNW rupture propagation offshore
properties.fault_geometry: Deep, throughgoing structure; dips gently with depth — supracrustal submarine fault system
